name: Power One
description_short: Power One Federal Credit Union is a full-service credit union established in 1935, primarily serving AEP employees and their families across Indiana and Michigan with competitive loan and savings products.
description_long:
Power One Federal Credit Union was established in 1935 as I & ME Federal Credit Union and has operated for 90 years with its home office in Fort Wayne, Indiana. The credit union primarily serves employees of American Electric Power (AEP) and their family members, though membership eligibility requirements apply. Over its history, Power One has expanded from its original Fort Wayne location to include branches in Rockport, Indiana (opened 1994 at an AEP coal plant) and Stevensville, Michigan (established 1999, relocated from Bridgman in 2007 to serve the Cook Nuclear Facility). The credit union positions itself as a full-service financial institution committed to providing personal service across multiple channels.

Power One offers a comprehensive range of banking products and services including savings and checking accounts, various loan products, VISA cards, and digital banking through their Member.Net platform. Loan products include new and used vehicle loans (as low as 5.59% APR), signature loans (as low as 9.99% APR), motorcycle/boat/RV loans (as low as 6.99% APR), and student loan partnerships. The credit union provides free ATM access to help members avoid fees, online and mobile banking applications for iOS and Android, and recently upgraded their digital banking platform as of June 2025 with enhanced security and convenience features.

Power One distinguishes itself through its focus on a specific membership base (AEP employees and families) rather than serving the general public, which allows for specialized service tailored to this demographic. The credit union has maintained a 90-year operational history rooted in a particular employer community, providing stability and institutional knowledge. Their June 2025 platform upgrade demonstrates ongoing investment in modern digital banking features including biometric login, secure messaging, card controls, real-time alerts, and remote deposit capture. The institution operates three physical branches across three states, providing in-person service options alongside digital channels.

Power One is best suited for AEP employees and their families seeking competitive loan rates and full-service banking from a long-established, stable institution. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted—the credit union primarily serves AEP employees and their family members rather than the general public. Rates are approval-based on creditworthiness and specific membership requirements. For those who qualify, the credit union offers solid competitive rates, though consumers should verify their eligibility before assuming membership is available. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: New vehicle loans (as low as 5.59% APR)
Used vehicle loans (as low as 5.59% APR)
Motorcycle, boat, and RV loans (as low as 6.99% APR)
Member.Net digital banking platform with biometric login
Remote deposit capture with real-time posting
pros: Competitive loan rates: new/used vehicle loans as low as 5.59% APR, signature loans as low as 9.99% APR
Free ATM access network eliminates out-of-network ATM fees for members
Modern digital banking platform upgraded June 2025 with biometric login and secure messaging
90-year operational history since 1935 provides institutional stability and experience
Full-service credit union offering savings, checking, loans, and VISA products in one institution
Remote deposit capture available for smartphone check deposits with real-time posting
Three physical branch locations across Indiana and Michigan for in-person service
Card controls feature allows members to manage debit and credit cards in real-time
cons: Membership is restricted to AEP employees and their family members—not available to general public
Limited geographic footprint with only three branches across two states
Student loans require partnership with third-party provider rather than direct credit union origination
Website provides limited information about savings account rates or CD products
No mention of credit counseling, financial education, or advisory services
best_for: AEP (American Electric Power) employees seeking competitive rates from their employer-affiliated credit union
Family members of AEP employees eligible for membership with various banking needs
Members in Allen County, Indiana or North Central Indiana/Michigan seeking personalized local banking
Borrowers looking for vehicle financing with competitive APR rates below market average
diagnosis: Power One Federal Credit Union is best for AEP employees and eligible family members seeking full-service banking with competitive loan rates and modern digital features. The critical caveat is that membership is restricted to this specific employer group and their families—general public consumers cannot join, making eligibility verification essential before considering this institution.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
